key_insight: EKSFT improves low-data supervised fine-tuning by masking tokens with high entropy or high KL divergence, so the model learns task-relevant skills without overfitting to noisy or distribution-shifting details.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical training strategy for math-focused LLM pipelines that preserves pre-trained behavior and leads to better downstream RL exploration and performance.
method: Compute token-level entropy and KL divergence against a reference model
Mask high-uncertainty or high-shift tokens during SFT imitation
Use the resulting model as a better initialization for RL fine-tuning
evidence_signal: On mathematical reasoning benchmarks, EKSFT consistently outperformed standard SFT, and models further RL-finetuned from EKSFT achieved better post-RL results.
watch_item: Token masking thresholds and reference-model choice may be sensitive hyperparameters, so improper calibration could suppress useful supervision or reduce gains.
paper_title: Entropy-KL Divergence-based Token Masking: A Novel Approach for Selective Fine-tuning of Large Language Models
